name: Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats)
name_original: El Barranco de la Viuda - Cala Llebeig & Moraig (SL-CV 50)
description: Hike the moderate 6.3-mile Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats) for stunning Mediterranean views, sea caves, and secluded coves.
region_intro: Hiking around Teulada offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from dramatic coastal cliffs and secluded coves to verdant inland vineyards and agricultural terraces. The region features well-maintained trails that navigate these varied terrains, providing views of the Mediterranean Sea and historical landmarks. Elevations are generally moderate, with coastal paths often reaching up to 200 meters. Best hiking trails around Teulada The most popular hiking route is Cap d'Or Watchtower Trail, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive coastal views and leads to a 16th-century watchtower. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats), a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail follows the dramatic coastline, providing views of the Mediterranean and access to coves. Local hikers also love the Cap d'Or Watchtower Circular from Moraira, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Hiking around Teulada is defined by coastal cliffs, picturesque coves, and inland vineyards. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Teulada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Teulada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Teulada?
A: Teulada offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find dramatic coastal cliffs, some reaching up to 200 meters, with breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. The area also features picturesque coves and beaches with crystal-clear waters. Inland, the scenery transforms into verdant vineyards and agricultural terraces, dotted with traditional 'riuraus' and historic windmills like the Molí dels Castellons.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Teulada?
A: Yes, Teulada has several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14676012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peñón de Ifach – Watchtower of Cap d'Or loop from Moraira&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km hike. The region generally offers a good selection of paths for various ability levels, with 45 easy tours available.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Teulada?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Teulada are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870113882/cap-d-or-watchtower-circular-from-moraira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap d'Or Watchtower Circular from Moraira&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal scenery and takes about 1 hour 45 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Teulada's trails lead to several captivating natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the unique geological formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/174441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifach summit&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the majestic stone arches of the Cova dels Arcs at Cala Moraig. The region also features the 16th-century watchtower at Cap d'Or and the 19th-century Ermita de la Font Santa with its miraculous fountain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Teulada?
A: Teulada benefits from mild temperatures for much of the year, making it an ideal destination for hiking. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant weather for exploration. Even in winter, conditions are generally favorable, allowing for comfortable walks. While summers can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable for coastal paths.

Q: Are there any trails that offer opportunities for wild swimming or access to secluded coves?
A: Yes, Teulada's coastline is dotted with picturesque coves and beaches perfect for swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870093945/cliffs-trail-ruta-dels-penya-segats" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides stunning Mediterranean views and access to several secluded coves like Cala Moraig, Cala Llebeig, and Cala Andragó, many with crystal-clear turquoise waters.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Teulada?
A: The hiking routes in Teulada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from dramatic coastal views to tranquil inland vineyards, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Teulada's trails often pass by or lead to interesting historical sites. You can find ancient 'riuraus' used for drying Muscat grapes in the agricultural areas, and the historic Molí dels Castellons windmill. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable landmark near some coastal routes.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Teulada?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead might be limited, Teulada and Moraira are connected by local bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes between Teulada town and coastal areas like Moraira, which can bring you closer to various starting points.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the popular hiking trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads, especially in areas like Moraira and close to key attractions such as Cap d'Or. However, during peak season, it's recommended to arrive early to secure a spot, particularly for well-known routes like the Cliffs Trail or those leading to specific coves.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While many trails are moderate, combining routes can create longer and more challenging experiences. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352252299/cliffs-trail-and-ermita-de-la-font-santa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs Trail and Ermita de la Font Santa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.9 km route with over 430 meters of elevation gain, offering a more extensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Teulada?
A: Teulada's diverse ecosystems, from coastal cliffs to inland vineyards, support various forms of wildlife. Along the coast, you might spot seabirds, and the clear waters of the coves are home to vibrant underwater microecosystems, making them excellent for snorkeling. Inland, you may encounter smaller mammals and a variety of bird species typical of Mediterranean scrubland and agricultural areas.
tips: A very picturesque route without technical difficulty, although it is recommended to wear suitable footwear to be able to travel without problems. If you want to get to the coves, avoid being heavily loaded or pushing a car, as you will end up carrying it on your shoulder and it will not be comfortable at all.
A beach and abandoned fishing huts. Great Place for Picnic!
Cova del Morro del Bou Circular route from Moraira.

It is a local trail that runs along the cliff, very simple and with hardly any unevenness. The views are beautiful. Ideal to enjoy with the family or take a quiet walk.

The trail is marked by white and green rock markers.

La Cova dels Arcs, as well as being a magical corner to watch a sunrise, take pictures or practice snorkeling (in the “dels Immersionistes” room, the one with easy access), has an area for expert speleologists capable of delving into which is considered the outlet to the sea of the longest groundwater course in Spain. It is located next to Cala del Moraig.

faqs.question: Where does the Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats) start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near Benitachell. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's advisable to look for designated parking areas close to the trailhead, especially if you plan to access the route from the northern end near Cala Moraig - Cova dels Arcs or the southern end towards Llebeig Cove. Arriving early is recommended to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The Cliffs Trail is generally considered moderate in difficulty. While not overly technical, some sections are rocky, narrow, and uneven, particularly the descent to Llebeig Cove. Hikers should have minimal experience and sure-footedness. It can be suitable for active families, but sturdy hiking shoes are essential.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cliffs Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by dramatic limestone cliffs and a path that is often rocky and narrow. You'll find uneven sections, but it's generally well-trodden. The route hugs the cliff face, offering constant views of the Mediterranean Sea and leading to secluded pebble beaches.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural and historical features. You'll encounter the impressive Barranco de la Viuda, the secluded Llebeig Cove, and several fascinating sea caves like Morro del Bou Cave and Cala Moraig - Cova dels Arcs. You'll also discover old fishing huts carved into the rock, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

faqs.question: Is the Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the narrow and rocky sections, especially on descents, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with uneven terrain. Always carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, as there are no services on the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is during the cooler months, from autumn to spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming Mediterranean flora. It's advisable to start early in the morning to avoid both heat and crowds, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats). It is a publicly accessible route along the coastline.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no facilities, shops, or natural water sources directly on the trail. It is essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, and anything else you might need for the duration of your hike. Plan to use facilities before or after your visit in nearby towns.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Penya-Segats Trail itself, which makes up a significant portion of the route. It also intersects with sections of the Pueblo Palmeria - Fisher bay trail.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The coastline is part of the Natura 2000 Network, a protected natural space. You can expect to see diverse Mediterranean flora, including rare and endemic plant species. As a Special Protection Area for Birds, you might spot various bird species. Keep an eye out for local wildlife adapted to the coastal environment.

faqs.question: Is the Cliffs Trail (Ruta dels Penya-segats) a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Cliffs Trail is primarily an out-and-back route. Many hikers choose Llebeig Cove as a turnaround point, offering a chance to swim before heading back the way they came.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the secluded Llebeig Cove is a major highlight and offers an excellent opportunity for a refreshing swim in its turquoise waters. It's a popular spot to take a break and cool off during your hike.
